The One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA) signed into law on July 4, 2025, introduces changes to the domestic content requirements for the Production Tax Credits (45Y) under the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA). According to Article 1, the OBBBA harmonizes the domestic content requirements under Section 48E with those under Section 45Y for projects beginning construction on or after June 16, 2025. This implies that meeting domestic-content rules will be mandatory to claim the 45Y Production Tax Credit. The changes indicate a shift towards emphasizing domestic sourcing and manufacturing in the energy sector.

The One Big Beautiful Bill Act, signed into law by President Trump, revises the domestic content requirements for the Investment Tax Credits (48E) under the Inflation Reduction Act. According to Article 1, the Act imposes specific domestic-content percentages for Section 48E projects, with thresholds ranging from 40% to 55% depending on the construction start date. Article 2 also mentions that the Senate Finance Committee's reconciliation bill updates the Section 48E domestic content rule for bonus credit eligibility, indicating a shift towards mandatory domestic content requirements. These changes suggest that domestic content requirements will become mandatory for obtaining the IRA's Investment Tax Credits (48E).

The House Reconciliation Bill Amends Clean Energy Provisions of the IRA, which includes amendments to the Advanced Manufacturing Production Tax Credit (45X) that introduce requirements for domestically-produced materials or subcomponents. According to Article 1, the legislation extends the Clean Fuel Production Credit through 2031 with a U.S., Canada, or Mexico feedstock requirement. Additionally, Article 2 mentions that fuel sold after December 31, 2025, would be required to be exclusively derived from feedstock produced or grown in the United States, Mexico, or Canada. These changes suggest that the use of domestic materials or subcomponents will be required for the Advanced Manufacturing Production Tax Credits (45X).
